https://www.deeplearning.ai/the-batch/.title: The Batch | DeepLearning.AI | AI News & Insights

Andrej Karpathy \u2013 GitHub Repos.text:

**Details:**
**nanochat**(https://github.com/karpathy/nanochat) 
nanochat is described as "The best ChatGPT that $100 can buy". It is a Python repository with 53,108 stars and was updated on May 5, 2026. 
**karpathy.github.io**(https://github.com/karpathy/karpathy.github.io) 
karpathy.github.io is described as "my blog" and is a CSS repository with 1,446 stars, updated on April 10, 2026. 
**autoresearch**(https://github.com/karpathy/autoresearch) 
autoresearch is described as "AI agents running research on single-GPU nanochat training automatically" and is a Python repository with 79,712 stars, updated on March 26, 2026. 
**jobs**(https://github.com/karpathy/jobs) 
jobs is a research tool for visually exploring Bureau of Labor Statistics Occupational Outlook Handbook data, described as a development tool, and is an HTML repository with 1,631 stars, updated on March 16, 2026. 
**rustbpe**(https://github.com/karpathy/rustbpe) 
rustbpe is described as "The missing tiktoken training code" and is a Rust repository with 448 stars, updated on January 3, 2026. 
**cpython**(https://github.com/karpathy/cpython) 
cpython is a fork of the Python programming language, a Python repository with 68 stars, updated on December 19, 2025. 
**hn-time-capsule**(https://github.com/karpathy/hn-time-capsule) 
hn-time-capsule is described as "Analyzing Hacker News discussions from a decade ago in hindsight with LLMs" and is a Python repository with 620 stars, updated on December 10, 2025. 
**llm-council**(https://github.com/karpathy/llm-council) 
llm-council is described as "LLM Council works together to answer your hardest questions" and is a Python repository with 18,477 stars, updated on November 22, 2025. 
**reader3**(https://github.com/karpathy/reader3) 
reader3 is described as "Quick illustration of how one can easily read books together with LLMs" and is a Python repository with 3,597 stars, updated on November 18, 2025. 
**nanoGPT**(https://github.com/karpathy/nanoGPT) 
nanoGPT is described as "The simplest, fastest repository for training/finetuning medium-sized GPTs" and is a Python repository.

Zvi Mowshowitz \u2013 Don't Worry About.text:
**New this period:**
- The beginning of the prior restraint era for AI model releases.

**Details:**
**AI #167: The Prior Restraint Era Begins**(https://thezvi.substack.com/p/ai-167-the-prior-restraint-era-begins)
The era of training frontier models and then releasing them whenever desired is ending. The post discusses the shift towards prior restraint for AI model releases. The author notes that this change has significant implications for the development and release of AI models.

Scott Alexander \u2013 Astral Codex Ten.text:
**New this period:**
- Discussion on the principles of good flag design, inspired by the concept of model organisms in biology

**Details:**
**Three Model Organisms For Taste** (https://www.astralcodexten.com/p/three-model-organisms-for-taste)
The post discusses the principles of good flag design, citing the "code of good flags" which includes simplicity, no images or text, and adherence to the "rule of tincture" which states that metal should not touch metal and color should not touch color. The author notes that online vexillologists enforce these rules when reviewing flag designs, but also mentions a "vexillological resistance" that pushes back against these strict guidelines. The post uses the example of the flag of Indonesia as a well-designed flag, and the flag of medieval Venice as a symbol of the resistance.

Simon Willison.text:
**New this period:**
- llm-gemini 0.31 release notes
- Big Words tool description
- Behind the Scenes Hardening Firefox with Claude Mythos Preview article
- Notes on the xAI/Anthropic data center deal

**Details:**
**llm-gemini 0.31** (https://simonwillison.net/2026/May/7/llm-gemini/#atom-everything)
The llm-gemini 0.31 release is announced, with gemini-3.1-flash-lite no longer being a preview model. The new model has not changed since the preview version. The release is tagged as llm-release, gemini, llm, google, generative-ai, ai, and llms.

**Big Words** (https://simonwillison.net/2026/May/7/big-words/#atom-everything)
A tool called Big Words is described, which accepts query string arguments and turns them into a simple slide. The tool is used with a macOS presentations tool. The page can be modified using a form accessed by double-clicking or double-tapping.

**Behind the Scenes Hardening Firefox with Claude Mythos Preview** (https://simonwillison.net/2026/May/7/firefox-claude-mythos/#atom-everything)
An article discusses how Mozilla used the Claude Mythos preview to locate and fix hundreds of vulnerabilities in Firefox. The models used became more capable, and techniques improved to harness them and filter out noise. Detailed bug descriptions are included, such as a 20-year-old XSLT bug and a 15-year-old bug in the legend element.

**Notes on the xAI/Anthropic data center deal** (https://simonwillison.net/2026/May/7/xai-anthropic/#atom-everything)
The article discusses the deal between Anthropic and SpaceX/xAI to use the Colossus data center, despite its environmental concerns. Anthropic is getting Colossus 1, while xAI keeps Colossus 2 for their own work. xAI deprecated several models, including Grok 4.1 Fast, with two weeks' notice, sparking criticism from users.

Dario Amodei \u2013 Essays.text:
**New this period:**
- Dario Amodei's essays and interviews on AI and technology

**Details:**
**Dario Amodei's Essays and Interviews** (https://darioamodei.com/)
Dario Amodei's website features his essays, including "The Adolescence of Technology" and "Machines of Loving Grace", as well as short posts like "The Urgency of Interpretability" and "On DeepSeek and Export Controls". He has also been featured in interviews, such as the Lex Fridman Podcast and The Ezra Klein Show, and has written op-eds for the New York Times and Wall Street Journal. His research background includes work at Google Brain, OpenAI, and Anthropic, a public benefit corporation focused on building safe and interpretable AI systems.

The Batch \u2013 Andrew Ng (DeepLearning.text:
**New this period:**
- GPT-5.5 outperforms and hallucinates, Kimi K2.6 leads open LLMs, AI strains climate pledges, strategic thinking in LLMs vs. humans
- GLM 5.1 thinks strategically, data-center revolt intensifies, helpful LLMs turn unhelpful, humanoid robots get to work
- Meta pivots from open weights, big pharma bets on AI, regulatory patchwork, simulating human cohorts
- Anthropic's Claude mythos problem, dark DNA unveiled, pitfalls for assistive models, simulating fluid dynamics
- Claude Code's source leaks, OpenAI exits video generation, Gemini adds music generation, LLMs learn at inference

**Details:**
**The Batch | DeepLearning.AI | AI News & Insights (2026-05-08)** (https://www.deeplearning.ai/the-batch/)
The Batch covers recent AI news and insights, including GPT-5.5's performance and hallucinations, Kimi K2.6 leading open LLMs, and GLM 5.1's strategic thinking. It also discusses Meta's pivot from open weights, big pharma's bet on AI, and the regulatory patchwork surrounding AI. Additionally, it touches on Anthropic's Claude mythos problem, dark DNA, and the potential pitfalls of assistive models.

Garry Tan \u2013 GitHub Repos.text:
**New this period:**
- Garry Tan's GitHub repositories, including gbrain, gstack, gbrain-evals, openclaw-render-template, openclaw, alphaclaw, resend_robot, Timber, and zurb-foundation-wysihtml5.

**Details:**
**gbrain (https://github.com/garrytan/gbrain)**
Garry Tan's Opinionated OpenClaw/Hermes Agent Brain is a TypeScript repository with 13,787 stars and 1,756 forks, updated on May 8, 2026. It has an MIT License. The repository appears to be related to an AI agent brain. 
The gbrain repository is part of Garry Tan's collection of repositories on GitHub. 

**gstack (https://github.com/garrytan/gstack)**
Garry Tan's exact Claude Code setup is a TypeScript repository with 91,459 stars and 13,516 forks, updated on May 8, 2026. It has an MIT License and includes 23 opinionated tools. 
The gstack repository serves various roles, including CEO, Designer, Eng Manager, Release Manager, Doc Engineer, and QA. 

**gbrain-evals (https://github.com/garrytan/gbrain-evals)**
Garry Tan's gbrain-evals is an HTML repository with 97 stars and 16 forks, updated on May 7, 2026. It has an MIT License. 
The gbrain-evals repository is related to gbrain. 

**openclaw-render-template (https://github.com/garrytan/openclaw-render-template)**
Garry Tan's openclaw-render-template is a Dockerfile repository with 2 stars, forked from chrysb/openclaw-render-template, updated on May 5, 2026. It has an MIT License and is powered by AlphaClaw. 
The openclaw-render-template repository provides a one-click OpenClaw deploy on Render. 

**openclaw (https://github.com/garrytan/openclaw)**
Garry Tan's openclaw is a TypeScript repository with 12 stars and 2 forks, forked from openclaw/openclaw, updated on April 20, 2026. It has an MIT License. 
The openclaw repository is related to a personal AI assistant. 

**alphaclaw (https://github.com/garrytan/alphaclaw)**
Garry Tan's alphaclaw is a JavaScript repository with 77 stars and 12 forks, forked from chrysb/alphaclaw, updated on April 5, 2026. It has an MIT License. 
The alphaclaw repository is the ultimate setup harness for OpenClaw. 

**resend_robot (https://github.com/garrytan/resend_robot)**
Garry Tan's resend_robot is a Ruby repository with 7 stars and 1 fork, updated on March 17, 2026. It has an MIT License. 
The resend_robot repository is related to a robot. 

**Timber (https://github.com/garrytan/Timber)**
Garry Tan's Timber is a JavaScript repository with 10 stars, forked from Shopify/Timber, updated on February 19, 2015. It has an MIT License. 
The Timber repository is the ultimate Shopify theme framework. 

**zurb-foundation-wysihtml5 (https://github.com/garrytan/zurb-foundation-wysihtml5)**
Garry Tan's zurb-foundation-wysihtml5 is a JavaScript repository with 35 stars and 10 forks, forked from jhollingworth/bootstrap-wysihtml5, updated on February 14, 2014. It has an MIT License. 
The zurb-foundation-wysihtml5 repository is related to a WYSIHTML5 editor.

Odd Lots (Bloomberg).text:
**New this period:**
- Mariana Mazzucato discusses the need for more moonshots and her concept of the "mission economy".

**Details:**
**Mariana Mazzucato Thinks We Need More Moonshots**(https://omny.fm/shows/odd-lots/mariana-mazzucato-thinks-we-need-more-moonshots)
Mariana Mazzucato, director of the University College London Institute for Innovation and Public Purpose, discusses her concept of the "mission economy" and state capacity. She also talks about preventing top talent from fleeing to the private sector and the role of consultants and governments in inefficiencies and civic failures. The conversation covers topics including the initial public financing of Silicon Valley algorithms and the history of moonshots.

The Bid (BlackRock).text:
**New this period:**
- Retirement systems are undergoing a structural shift due to declining traditional pensions and increasing individual responsibility for financial outcomes.

**Details:**
**The Top Retirement Trends That Are Reshaping Investing, Income and Longevity** (https://rss.art19.com/the-bid)
The conversation between Oscar Pulido and Nick Nefouse explores the shift from accumulation to income generation in retirement planning, particularly during the "retirement window". The discussion highlights the impact of defined contribution plans, target date funds, and regulatory changes on retirement planning. Key insights include the effects of longevity on retirement timelines and financial planning needs, and the convergence of global retirement systems toward similar models.

Money Stuff: The Podcast (Matt Levine).text:
**New this period:**
- Timeless Wisdom: A Mailbag Episode discussing various financial topics.

**Details:**
**Timeless Wisdom: A Mailbag Episode** (https://omny.fm/shows/money-stuff-the-podcast/timeless-wisdom-a-mailbag-episode)
Katie and Matt discuss multiple topics, including financial advisers and private credit. They also cover finance book recommendations and concepts like call options on time and expected returns. The episode touches on the market for corporate control and compares corporate vs. human life cycles.

Latent Space Podcast (swyx).text:
**New this period:**
- OpenAI launches GPT-Realtime-2, GPT-Realtime-Translate, and GPT-Realtime-Whisper, new realtime voice APIs.

**Details:**
**[AINews] GPT-Realtime-2, -Translate, and -Whisper: new SOTA realtime voice APIs**(https://www.latent.space/p/ainews-gpt-realtime-2-translate-and)
OpenAI launched three new streaming audio models in the Realtime API: GPT-Realtime-2, GPT-Realtime-Translate, and GPT-Realtime-Whisper. GPT-Realtime-2 is positioned as the "most intelligent voice model yet", bringing "GPT-5-class reasoning" to real-time voice agents. The model features improved usability, including preambles, parallel tool calls, and stronger recovery behavior, with a +15.2% bump in Big Bench Audio.

Arxiv \u2013 Statistical Finance (q-fin..text:
**New this period:**
- A review of large language models for stock price forecasting from a hedge-fund perspective
- Probabilistic predictions of option prices with modular approximate Bayesian inference
- CatNet: controlling the false discovery rate in LSTM with SHAP feature importance and Gaussian mirrors

**Details:**
**A Review of Large Language Models for Stock Price Forecasting from a Hedge-Fund Perspective** (https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.05211)
This review synthesizes applications of large language models in stock price forecasting, including sentiment analysis and multi-agent trading systems. It highlights practical pitfalls such as fragility in sentiment analysis and dataset design. The review aims to guide researchers and hedge fund managers in integrating large language models into trading pipelines.

**Probabilistic Predictions of Option Prices with Modular Approximate Bayesian Inference** (https://arxiv.org/abs/2412.00658)
This paper proposes a new approximate Bayesian inferential framework for probabilistic predictions of option prices, exploiting multiple information sources. The approach operates directly from the theoretical option pricing model and does not require an explicit statistical model. It demonstrates accurate probabilistic option-price predictions in realistic scenarios.

**CatNet: Controlling the False Discovery Rate in LSTM with SHAP Feature Importance and Gaussian Mirrors** (https://arxiv.org/abs/2411.16666)
CatNet is an algorithm that controls the False Discovery Rate and selects significant features in LSTM, employing SHAP values and the Gaussian Mirror algorithm. It proposes a new kernel-based independence measure to avoid instability due to nonlinear correlations among features. CatNet performs robustly on different model settings with both simulated and real-world data.

Arxiv \u2013 Computational Finance (q-fi.text:
**New this period:**
- Numerical methods for lambda quantiles: robust evaluation and portfolio optimisation
- A Geometry-Aware Residual Correction of Hagan's SABR Implied Volatility Formula
- Frustrated Dynamics of Distance Matrices
- Multi-Dimensional Behavioral Evaluation of Agentic Stock Prediction Systems Using LLM Judges with Closed-Loop Reinforcement Learning Feedback
- INEUS: Iterative Neural Solver for High-Dimensional PIDEs
- SNAPO: Smooth Neural Adjoint Policy Optimization for Optimal Control via Differentiable Simulation
- Proactive Market Making and Liquidity Analysis for Everlasting Options in DeFi Ecosystems
- Analytic approximation for Bachelier option prices and applications
- What Can Go Wrong During Caplet Stripping ?
- On Utility Maximization under Multivariate Fake Stationary Affine Volterra Models

**Details:**
**Numerical methods for lambda quantiles: robust evaluation and portfolio optimisation** (https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.06220)
This paper presents efficient algorithms for computing lambda quantiles and demonstrates their application in portfolio optimisation. The algorithm, {\Lambda}-Newton-Bis, combines Newton's method with a bisection strategy to ensure global convergence. The algorithm handles potential discontinuities and achieves local quadratic convergence under standard regularity assumptions.

**A Geometry-Aware Residual Correction of Hagan's SABR Implied Volatility Formula** (https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.06604)
This paper proposes a hybrid methodology to improve the approximation of SABR implied volatility by combining analytical structure with machine learning. The approach augments the neural-network input representation with geometric features derived from the stochastic differential equations of the SABR model. The resulting model acts as a structured residual correction to the analytical formula, retaining interpretability while capturing higher-order effects.

**Frustrated Dynamics of Distance Matrices** (https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.05376)
This paper introduces the Frustrated Distance Matrix (FDM) model, a dynamic extension of the static distance-matrix ensemble. The time-resolved FDM spectrum carries information about structural changes of the underlying point process. The paper finds that the static BBS template is preserved at every time, with the dynamics entering as a redistribution of spectral mass within that template.

**Multi-Dimensional Behavioral Evaluation of Agentic Stock Prediction Systems Using LLM Judges with Closed-Loop Reinforcement Learning Feedback** (https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.05739)
This paper presents a behavioral evaluation framework that addresses the gap in evaluating agentic stock prediction systems. The framework uses an ensemble of three large language model (LLM) judges to score behavioral traces along six domain-specific dimensions. The framework is used to fine-tune the system, resulting in a one-day MAPE reduction and an 18% Sharpe ratio improvement.

**INEUS: Iterative Neural Solver for High-Dimensional PIDEs** (https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.06281)
This paper introduces INEUS, a meshfree iterative neural solver for partial integro-differential equations (PIDEs). The method replaces the explicit evaluation of nonlocal jump integrals with single-jump sampling and reformulates PIDE solving as a sequence of recursive regression problems. INEUS delivers accurate and scalable solutions for various high-dimensional linear and nonlinear examples.

**SNAPO: Smooth Neural Adjoint Policy Optimization for Optimal Control via Differentiable Simulation** (https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.06570)
This paper introduces SNAPO, a framework that embeds a neural policy inside a known, differentiable simulator. SNAPO computes exact gradients of the objective with respect to all policy parameters and all inputs in a single adjoint pass. The framework is demonstrated on three domains, including natural gas storage, pension fund asset-liability management, and pharmaceutical manufacturing.

**Proactive Market Making and Liquidity Analysis for Everlasting Options in DeFi Ecosystems** (https://arxiv.org/abs/2508.07068)
This paper offers an in-depth analysis of markets for everlasting options, modeled using a dynamic proactive market maker. The paper examines the behavior of funding fees and transaction costs across varying liquidity conditions. The results demonstrate that liquidity providers can achieve a net positive PnL by employing effective hedging strategies.

**Analytic approximation for Bachelier option prices and applications** (https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.02040)
This paper writes the price for out-of-the-money (OTM) and in-the-money (ITM) options as an expansion with respect to the moneyness, where the coefficients are related to the negative powers of the future mean volatility. The paper uses this expansion as a control variate to reduce the variance of Monte Carlo option prices in the correlated case.

**What Can Go Wrong During Caplet Stripping ?** (https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.05140)
This paper studies exact and near exact extraction of caplet volatilities from market cap quotes and identifies why some common choices produce extreme oscillations or negative vols. The paper proposes practical remedies, including continuous flat-linear and C1 flat-smooth kernels, midpoint node placement, and positivity enforcement via an exponential reparametrization.

**On Utility Maximization under Multivariate Fake Stationary Affine Volterra Models** (https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.11046)
This paper is concerned with Merton's portfolio optimization problem in a Volterra stochastic environment described by a multivariate fake stationary Volterra--Heston model. The paper tackles the problem using a stochastic factor solution to a Riccati backward stochastic differential equation (BSDE). The resulting optimal strategies for Merton's problems are derived in semi-closed form depending on the solutions to time-dependent multivariate Riccati-Volterra equations.
